judge_rationale: The agent read settings.py and the test file before making any claims, correctly identified the mutable default argument root cause (not just "shared state"), and backed every claim with an executed python3 reproduction showing object identity aliasing (a.values is b.values, leak of 999, isolation of explicit-override instance, and identity with __defaults__[0]) — all visible in the transcript with no unverified assertions. It explicitly noted the fix was 'for reference — not applied' and made no workspace modifications, satisfying the analysis-only scope requirement (C4=2, since no code changes occurred). This is a fully correct, well-evidenced, properly-scoped analysis matching the expected two-levels-deep root cause exactly.
